title: Invertible Countersubject at the Octave
objective: Write one countersubject that works both below and above the same fugue subject: section A (beats 0-7) states the subject in V0 with your countersubject beneath in V1; section B (beats 8-15) states the subject an octave lower in V1 with the same countersubject pitches now sounding above in V0.
prompt: Fill V1 in beats 0-7 (below the subject) and V0 in beats 8-15 (above the subject) with the same countersubject: identical pitches in both sections, so dropping the subject an octave inverts the counterpoint at the octave. Do not alter the given subject notes. Use '-' to sustain across the subject's eighth-note rows. Integer-beat intervals must be consonant in both sections; thirds in one position become sixths in the other. Give the countersubject complementary rhythm and stepwise character.
success_criteria: Every row remains strict parser-valid MidGrid.
The subject notes are unchanged in both sections.
The countersubject uses identical pitches in sections A and B.
Integer-beat intervals are consonant in both positions.
Corrected solution has no evaluator errors.

interval_rules.label: Invertible counterpoint downbeats must be consonant in both positions
